title: Homebrew formula for distribution
description: Homebrew formula for installing agentsight. Should also install the CC skill file (agentsight-0ms) so the feedback loop works out of the box. Depends on: agentsight-0ms (skill must exist to bundle it).

title: CC skill: /agentsight-diagnose for guided workflow improvement
description:
Create a Claude Code skill that developers install alongside agentsight. Invoked via /agentsight-diagnose in CC.

## How CC Skills Work

A skill is a markdown file with optional YAML frontmatter. CC auto-discovers files in:
- ~/.claude/commands/<name>.md (user-level, legacy format — simplest)
- ~/.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md (user-level, newer format — supports subdirectory with supporting files)

Skills can run shell commands as preprocessing using !-backtick syntax. The command output is embedded in the prompt before Claude sees it. This is how the skill feeds agentsight data to CC.

## Skill Content

File: ~/.claude/commands/agentsight-diagnose.md (or ~/.claude/skills/agentsight-diagnose/SKILL.md)

The skill will:
1. Run agentsight diagnose on the current project via !-backtick preprocessing
2. Read the project's CLAUDE.md via !-backtick preprocessing
3. Provide instructions for CC to interpret the diagnostics and CLAUDE.md together
4. Guide CC to produce specific, actionable recommendations

Key design: the heavy analysis (cache stability, context growth, tool patterns) happens in the Rust CLI. The skill just feeds that structured output to CC and tells it how to interpret the results and suggest CLAUDE.md changes.

## Argument Support

- /agentsight-diagnose — diagnose current project (auto-detected from cwd)
- /agentsight-diagnose <project-filter> — diagnose specific project
- Uses $ARGUMENTS variable for parameter passing

## Installation

Two options:
1. agentsight install-skill — CLI subcommand that copies the skill .md to ~/.claude/commands/
2. Homebrew post-install — brew formula copies skill file during install (see agentsight-0in)

The skill file is embedded in the agentsight binary (via rust_embed or include_str!) so it's always bundled with the correct version.

## Prerequisites

- agentsight binary must be in $PATH (for !-backtick preprocessing to work)
- CC must be able to find the skill file in ~/.claude/commands/ or ~/.claude/skills/

## Depends on

- agentsight-nwk (session-level diagnose command — provides the data)
- agentsight-6kp (project-level diagnose with --with-context — provides CLAUDE.md analysis)

## Reference

See docs/actionable-decisions.md for the kinds of decisions this enables.
See existing skills at ~/.claude/commands/ (build.md, test.md) for format examples.
close_reason: Implemented install-skill subcommand and /agentsight-diagnose skill. Skill auto-scopes to current project via --project flag. Uses include_str! embedding, --list/--force flags, JSON output support.

title: Diagnose: detect repeated identical Bash commands and same-error retries
description:
Enhance the Bash loop detector in diagnose to go beyond 'consecutive Bash-only turns' and detect two deeper anti-patterns:

1. **Identical command retries**: Extract the command string from ToolUse.input.command for Bash calls. Flag when the same command (or near-identical command) is repeated 3+ times across consecutive turns — the agent is retrying without changing its approach.

2. **Same-error retries**: Cross-reference assistant ToolUse blocks with the subsequent user entry's tool_use_result. Flag when consecutive Bash turns produce the same error output — the agent is getting the same error but not adapting.

## Data availability

Both signals are already in the JSONL data:
- Assistant entries: ContentBlock::ToolUse { name: "Bash", input: Value } — input contains a `command` field with the shell command string
- User entries: tool_use_result: Option<Value> — contains the tool output including error messages

## Implementation notes

- The current TurnSummary only stores tool names (Vec<String>). This enhancement requires either:
  (a) Enriching TurnSummary to carry tool inputs/outputs (heavier but reusable), or
  (b) Doing a second pass over raw SessionEntry data in the diagnose command specifically
- Option (b) is simpler and keeps the parser layer lean
- For 'same error' detection, compare normalized error strings (strip timestamps, paths, line numbers)
- The existing BashLoop struct could be extended with a `pattern` field: RetryLoop | IdenticalCommand | SameError

## Motivation

Real-world example: CC ran `pulumi stack output` 20+ times from the wrong directory, getting the same 'no Pulumi.yaml found' error each time. The current detector flags this as Bash loops, but richer detection would produce a more actionable recommendation like 'Agent repeated the exact same command 15 times — add the correct working directory to CLAUDE.md.'

title: diagnose dashboard page
description:
Add a Diagnose page to the web dashboard that surfaces the same analysis as the CLI diagnose command.

- Per-session diagnostics shown on session detail page (cache stability chart, context growth chart, tool pattern flags, recommendations panel)
- Project-level diagnostics on a new Diagnose page (cross-project comparison table, per-project trend sparklines, recommendation cards)
- Visual indicators: color-coded flags (green/yellow/red) for cache stability, context growth, tool patterns
- Recommendations panel with actionable text — same content as CLI output

API endpoints:
- GET /api/v1/diagnose/session/:id — session-level diagnostics JSON
- GET /api/v1/diagnose/project?project=<filter>&days=<N> — project-level diagnostics JSON

Depends on: agentsight-nwk, agentsight-6kp (CLI diagnose must exist first, dashboard visualizes same data).

title: Cross-session pattern detection (pillar 4)
description: Cross-session pattern detection — identify recurring patterns across sessions (repeated context churn, tool usage anti-patterns, project-level efficiency trends). See docs/actionable-decisions.md for cross-session decision framework and SPEC.md for project vision.
close_reason: Superseded. Cross-project comparison folded into diagnose command. Cross-session pattern detection is now a concrete feature within project-level diagnostics, not a standalone epic.

title: Context effectiveness scoring (pillar 2)
description: Context effectiveness scoring — help developers understand whether tokens were well spent, not just where they went. See docs/actionable-decisions.md for the gap analysis and SPEC.md for project vision. Key question: can we derive actionable quality signals from JSONL session data?
close_reason: Superseded. Replaced by diagnose command epic and concrete task beads. The abstract 'scoring' concept is now broken into specific diagnostics: cache stability, context growth, tool patterns, CLAUDE.md analysis.

title: Add session slug matching for `session` command
close_reason: Slug matching now uses case-insensitive substring match and returns most recent session when multiple share a slug. Dashboard by-slug endpoint updated with same logic.

title: Add daily/hourly token burn rate to summary
close_reason: Added hourly burn rate to summary: active hours, avg tokens/hour, peak hour. Computed from per-turn timestamps. Added to CLI, JSON, API, and dashboard summary page.

title: diagnose command: project-level comparison and CLAUDE.md analysis
description:
Extend diagnose command with project-level analysis: agentsight diagnose --project <filter> [--days 30] [--with-context] [--json]

1. **Cross-project benchmarks** — Compare cache efficiency, avg tokens/session, tool patterns across all projects. Rank projects by efficiency. "Project X averages 62% cache hit vs your overall 85%. This project likely needs better CLAUDE.md or more focused sessions."

2. **Project trend analysis** — Are sessions in this project getting more efficient over time, or degrading? Track cache hit ratio, tokens/session, and context growth trends across recent sessions. "Your last 5 sessions in project X show declining cache efficiency — from 90% to 71%. Something changed."

3. **CLAUDE.md impact analysis (--with-context)** — When flag is set, reads the project's CLAUDE.md (and any referenced .md files) and includes:
   - Estimated token cost of CLAUDE.md per turn (size in tokens)
   - Correlation: did sessions after the last CLAUDE.md change show better or worse cache efficiency?
   - Flag oversized CLAUDE.md: "Your CLAUDE.md is ~8K tokens. This adds ~8K to every turn's input. Consider trimming or splitting into focused files."
   - Flag missing CLAUDE.md: "No CLAUDE.md found for this project. Adding project-specific instructions can reduce exploration reads and improve cache efficiency."

4. **Output designed for CC consumption** — The text output is explicitly formatted so a developer can paste it into a CC conversation and say "here's my agentsight report, help me improve." Includes the CLAUDE.md content alongside the diagnostics so CC has everything it needs to suggest changes.

Depends on: agentsight-nwk (session-level diagnose). The project-level command aggregates session-level diagnostics.

See docs/actionable-decisions.md for the decision framework this enables.

title: Build `timeline` command for concurrent session visualization
close_reason: Implemented timeline CLI command with ASCII Gantt, dashboard page with interactive Gantt/concurrency/burn-rate charts, and /api/v1/timeline endpoint. Token attribution uses per-turn timestamps to avoid false burn during idle gaps.

title: Build test fixtures from real session data
description: Build sanitized test fixtures from real session data. See SPEC.md section 5 (Testing Strategy) for fixture requirements: replace code content with placeholders, keep token counts/timestamps/model names/tool names/UUIDs.

title: Differentiate same-slug sessions in watch view
description:
When Claude Code switches from plan mode to implementation (or resumes), it creates a new JSONL file but reuses the same slug. The watch view (both CLI and dashboard) then shows multiple rows with identical session names but different token counts, turns, and cache values. The older session is effectively dead but there's no way to tell.

## Options to explore

1. **Session ID prefix** — show a short UUID prefix alongside the slug (e.g., `delegated-waddling-bird (abc1)` vs `delegated-waddling-bird (def2)`)
2. **Start time column** — add session start time to disambiguate same-slug sessions
3. **Sequence number** — append an auto-incrementing number when multiple sessions share a slug (e.g., `delegated-waddling-bird #1`, `#2`)
4. **Dim/collapse stale sessions** — visually subordinate sessions that haven't changed for longer than active_window (still listed but greyed out or collapsed)
5. **Hybrid** — combine approaches, e.g. dim stale + show start time on hover in dashboard

## Context

Observed in CLI watch output:

```
| personal/agentsight | delegated-waddling-bird | 21,574,039 | 262 | 98.2% | idle 2m 54s |
| personal/agentsight | delegated-waddling-bird |  1,624,873 |  36 | 81.1% | active      |
```

The first row is from the plan mode session (never coming back), the second is the active implementation session.

## Applies to

- `agentsight watch` CLI command
- Dashboard watch page (SSE-based)
- Dashboard sessions list (less critical since sorted by date)
close_reason: Watch CLI and dashboard disambiguate same-slug sessions with short ID prefix. Dashboard watch adds Started column.

title: Add `--sort tokens` to sessions command
close_reason: Already implemented: sessions command has --sort tokens via SortField::Tokens

title: diagnose command: session-level analysis engine
description:
New CLI command: agentsight diagnose [<session-slug>] [--project <filter>] [--days <N>] [--json]

When given a session (or defaulting to most recent), produces a structured diagnostic report with:

1. **Cache stability analysis** — Per-turn cache_creation as % of total input. Flag sessions where cache creation stays >30% past turn 5 (context never stabilized). Classify as: stable (front-loads then drops), churning (stays high), or degrading (rises over time).

2. **Context growth detection** — Track per-turn input token trend. Linear growth = context bloating toward compaction. Flag when input tokens per turn grow >2x from turn 5 to final turn.

3. **Tool pattern flags** — Detect anti-patterns from per-turn tool data:
   - **Bash loop detection**: 3+ consecutive turns where the only tool is Bash = agent stuck retrying failed commands. This is one of the most expensive anti-patterns — burns tokens running the same broken command repeatedly. Recommendation: "Add known-good build/test commands to CLAUDE.md with expected output. Add a rule: if a command fails twice, stop and ask the user."
   - **Exploration without progress**: >5:1 Read:Edit ratio = agent reading extensively but producing little. Recommendation: "Point the agent at specific files in your prompt. Add a project map to CLAUDE.md listing key source files and their purpose."
   - **High Task/subagent spawns**: Each subagent loads its own context window. Recommendation: "Consider whether subagents are necessary or if direct tool calls would use fewer tokens."
   - Each flag includes a plain-english explanation of what it means and what the developer should do differently.

4. **Actionable recommendations** — Sentences, not numbers:
   - "This session's context never stabilized. Cache creation was >30% on 15 of 20 turns. Break multi-topic work into focused sessions."
   - "Input tokens grew 3.2x over this session. Start a new session before context compaction hits."
   - "Detected 4 Bash-only turn sequences (12 turns total). The agent was likely stuck retrying failed commands. Add error recovery guidance to your CLAUDE.md."
   - "High Read:Edit ratio (8:1). The agent spent most of its time exploring. Give more specific file paths in your prompts."

Output formats:
- Human-readable text (default) — designed to be pasted into a CC conversation for further analysis
- JSON (--json) — structured for programmatic consumption and skill integration

Implementation note: Tool names are captured per-turn in TurnSummary.tools. Bash command inputs are NOT currently parsed (only tool names). Sequential Bash-only turns are a reliable proxy for retry loops even without command text. Future enhancement could parse tool_use input blocks to detect identical repeated commands.

Depends on: existing parser, cost, and session infrastructure. No new parsing needed — all signals derivable from SessionSummary + TurnSummary.

See docs/actionable-decisions.md for the gap analysis that motivated this.
close_reason: Diagnose command fully implemented with cache stability, context growth, tool pattern analysis (bash loops, exploration ratio, subagent count), and actionable recommendations. Both text and JSON output. --project filter now works correctly.

title: Implement JSON output for `summary` command
close_reason: Already implemented: summary command has --json flag and JSON output path

title: Config file first-run experience
close_reason: Creates ~/.agentsight/config.toml with commented defaults on first run. Explains billing mode and model pricing overrides.

title: Onboarding report: agentsight init for new CC users
description:
New command: agentsight init [--project <filter>]

A first-run experience for new agentsight users that:

1. Scans all existing CC sessions (or a filtered project)
2. Produces a one-time baseline report:
   - Total sessions, tokens, and time spent in CC
   - Per-project efficiency ranking (cache hit, tokens/session, tool patterns)
   - Top 3 actionable recommendations based on historical data
   - Identifies the project that would benefit most from attention
3. Suggests next steps: "Run 'agentsight diagnose --project X --with-context' to get specific recommendations for your least efficient project"

This is the entry point for new users. Instead of dropping them into a dashboard with numbers they don't understand, it gives them a narrative: "Here's how you've been using CC, here's where you're doing well, here's where you can improve, and here's the next command to run."

Depends on: agentsight-nwk (uses the same diagnostic engine).

title: Session replay and debugging (pillar 3)
description: Session replay and debugging — enable developers to understand agent decision-making across turns. See docs/actionable-decisions.md for turn-level analysis patterns and SPEC.md for project vision.
close_reason: Canned. Turn-by-turn data already exists in session detail. Full replay is a visualization project that doesn't change developer behavior. Resources better spent on diagnostic/recommendation tooling.

title: Handle `<synthetic>` model gracefully
close_reason: Parser filters <synthetic> from per-turn model data. shorten_model maps it to unknown as safety net in both CLI and dashboard.
